# eFlash FTL

> A lightweight, production-grade Flash Translation Layer (FTL) library in portable C11 for embedded systems, microcontrollers, and IoT devices. No external dependencies, zero dynamic memory allocation.

## When to Use This Library

- You need wear leveling, garbage collection, and power-loss recovery on raw NAND/NOR/SPI flash
- You are on an embedded system (bare-metal, RTOS) with no heap available
- You need atomic write transactions with rollback capability
- You need ECC (BCH 3-bit) for flash bit errors

## Key Files

- eflash_ftl/eflash.h: SINGLE public API header. This is the only file you #include. Contains all public functions, constants, and data structures.
- eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl.c: Core FTL engine (~4000 lines). Contains Radix Tree, GC, transactions, power-loss recovery, code region management, write-back cache.
- eflash_ftl/eflash_mgr.c: Space manager. Free list with dynamic extension, page allocation, adjacent block merging.
- eflash_ftl/eflash_sim.c: Flash simulator for testing. Replace with your hardware driver for production.
- ecc/bch.c: BCH ECC encoder/decoder (3-bit correction).
- ecc/gf13.c: Galois Field GF(2^13) implementation.

## Architecture (4 Layers)

```
Application ˇú Public API (eflash.h) ˇú FTL Core (eflash_ftl.c) ˇú ECC Layer ˇú Hardware
                                           ˇý
                                     Space Manager (eflash_mgr.c)
```

## Core Data Structures

- Radix Tree: 16-level MSB-first tree mapping logical sector ˇú physical page. O(16) lookup.
- GC Ring Buffer: head (next writable) and tail (next to scan) pointers circling through physical pages.
- Transaction: shadow_root mechanism. Pages marked BLANKˇúREADYˇúCOMMITTED (or INVALID on abort).
- Write-Back Cache: 4-slot LRU, evicts to flash when full or on transaction commit.
- Free List: linked list of free physical pages, supports 4-level extension.

## Public API (all in eflash.h)

### Lifecycle
- eflash_init(filename) / eflash_deinit() ˇŞ flash simulation
- eflash_ftl_init() / eflash_get_ftl() ˇŞ global FTL instance (static, no malloc)

### Core I/O
- eflash_ftl_read(sector_id, data) ˇŞ read user data (464 bytes)
- eflash_ftl_write(sector_id, data) ˇŞ write-through (direct to flash)
- eflash_ftl_write_back(sector_id, data) ˇŞ cached write (write-back)

### Transactions
- eflash_ftl_txn_begin() / _commit() / _abort() ˇŞ atomic write
- eflash_ftl_txn_commit_with_update(sector, offset, word) ˇŞ optimized small update

### Garbage Collection
- eflash_ftl_gc_collect_once() / _full() / _emergency()
- eflash_ftl_gc_get_available_pages()

### Code Region (firmware storage)
- eflash_ftl_code_migrate_from_logical(src, count) ˇŞ move code to dedicated region
- eflash_ftl_code_expand() / _shrink() / _delete_segment()
- eflash_ftl_code_read() / _recover()

### Object Headers
- eflash_ftl_obj_set_header() / _get_header() / _free_header()

## Tests (73 total, 100% pass)

- 26 basic tests: read, write, GC, ECC, transactions, recovery, cache
- 19 code region tests: migration, expansion, shrinking, power-loss during migration
- 27 extension tests: extreme power-loss, max capacity, emergency GC, edge cases
- 1 stability test: 100K random ops + 10 power cycles + final verification

## Known Bugs Fixed (2026-06-03)

- P1: GC head==tail branch ˇŞ valid pages were skipped without migration (eflash_ftl.c:2593)
- P2: write_back ignored write_through error return (eflash_ftl.c:1835)

## How to Port

Replace 4 functions in eflash_sim.c:
- eflash_hw_erase(page_addr)
- eflash_hw_prog(page_addr, data)
- eflash_hw_read(page_addr, data)
- eflash_hw_word_update(page_addr, offset, data)

## Key Constants

- EFLASH_PAGE_SIZE: 512 bytes
- EFLASH_TOTAL_PAGES: 2048 (1MB total)
- USER_DATA_SIZE: 464 bytes (per page)
- RADIX_DEPTH: 16
- META_SIZE: 48 bytes
- ECC_SIZE: 5 bytes (BCH)

## Documentation Files

- README.md: Project overview, quick start, API reference (English)
- DESIGN_OVERVIEW.md: Architecture, algorithms, data structures, recovery (Chinese)
- CHANGELOG.md: Version history
- TEST_SUMMARY.md: Test results
- BUGFIX_ROUND_20260603.md: Bug fix details
- CODE_CORRECTNESS_ANALYSIS.md: Code review report
